concept.shortdesc: When transporting the lift truck by truck or rail, lower the forks and tilt the

conbody.p: When transporting the lift truck by truck or rail, lower the forks and tilt the mast forward. Secure the lift truck with chains and binders.
The tie-down points are indicated on the lift truck frame. Use only the designated tie-down points.
Minimum chain size for tie-down: Grade 70 transport chain, 3/8 inch or equivalent.
Ensure the engine is off and the parking brake is applied. Remove the ignition key before transport.
Cover the exhaust outlet to prevent moisture and debris ingress during transport.
If transporting by crane, use only the designated lifting points. Never lift the truck by the mast or forks.
Page 342 of 500 | CD100S-7 / CD120S-7 / CD140S-7 / CD160S-7 Operation & Maintenance Manual | DL06P Tier-4
Always follow all safety precautions when operating or maintaining this equipment.
Ensure all operators are properly trained and certified before operating the lift truck.
Regular maintenance is essential to safe and efficient operation of the lift truck.
Contact your local CROWN dealer for parts, service, and technical support.
Keep this manual with the lift truck at all times for reference by operators and technicians.
Record all service and maintenance activities in the maintenance logbook.
Only use genuine CROWN replacement parts to maintain warranty and performance.
